zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Welles於大約1327年出生在英國林可爾史爾時，她的父親Adam de Welles 3rd Lord Welles24歲，母親Margaret Bardolf16歲。她於大約1341年與William Deincourt II在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1402年5月29日在英國林可爾史爾布藍可尼享年76歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Welles于大约1327年出生在英国林可爾史爾时，她的父亲Adam de Welles 3rd Lord Welles24岁，母亲Margaret Bardolf16岁。她于大约1341年与William Deincourt II在英国英格兰林可爾史爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1402年5月29日在英国林可爾史爾布藍可尼享年76岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Welles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Wells
meanings: English: habitational name from Wells next the Sea (Norfolk) or Wells (Somerset), both named with the plural of Old English wella ‘spring, stream’, or a topographic name for someone who lived near a group of springs or streams.
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Dupuis   ‘from the well’.
History: One of numerous early immigrants from England bearing this name was Thomas Welles, governor of colonial CT, who was in Hartford, CT, by 1636.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Welles was born about 1327, in Lincolnshire, England, her father, Adam de Welles 3rd Lord Welles, was 24 and her mother, Margaret Bardolf, was 16. She married William Deincourt II about 1341, in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 29 May 1402, in Blankney, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 76.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Welles est née environ 1327, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Adam de Welles 3rd Lord Welles, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Margaret Bardolf, avait 16 ans. Elle a épousé William Deincourt II environ 1341, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 29 mai 1402, à Blankney, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 76 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Welles um 1327 in Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Adam de Welles 3rd Lord Welles 24 und ihre Mutter Margaret Bardolf 16. Sie hat um 1341 in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom William Deincourt II geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 29. Mai 1402 im Alter von 76 Jahren in Blankney, Lincolnshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Welles è nata il circa 1327 nel Lincolnshire, England, sua padre, Adam de Welles 3rd Lord Welles, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Margaret Bardolf, aveva 16 anni. Lei sposato William Deincourt II circa 1341, nel Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 29 maggio 1402, nel Blankney, Lincolnshire, England, all'età di 76 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Wellesが約1327年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Adam de Welles 3rd Lord Wellesは24歳，母Margaret Bardolfは16歳であった。約1341年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアでWilliam Deincourt IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1402年5月29日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアブランクニーで76歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Welles(이)가 대략 1327년 영국 링코실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Adam de Welles 3rd Lord Welles(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Bardolf(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 대략 1341년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르에서 William Deincourt II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1402년 5월 29일 영국 링코실르 블랭니에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Welles nasceu aproximadamente 1327, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Adam de Welles 3rd Lord Welles, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Bardolf, tinha 16 anos. Ela casou-se com William Deincourt II aproximadamente 1341, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 29 de maio de 1402, em Blankney, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, com 76 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Welles родилась около 1327 г. в местности линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Adam de Welles 3rd Lord Welles было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Bardolf, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Deincourt II около 1341 г. в местности линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 29 мая 1402 г. в местности бланни, линколншир, Англия в возрасте 76 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de Welles nació aproximadamente 1327, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Adam de Welles 3rd Lord Welles, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Margaret Bardolf, tenía 16 años. Se casó con William Deincourt II aproximadamente 1341, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 29 de mayo de 1402, en Blankney, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 76 años.
